home.name: Raffael de Castro Rodrigues
home.headline: I build applications across their whole life-cycle — from architecture to delivery —, design and optimize workflows, and create, manage and mentor software development teams.

about.title: About Me
about.lead: Lead software engineer across the whole software life-cycle — idealization, architecture, development, debugging and optimization.
about.paragraph1: I'm Raffael, a Brazilian software engineer based in Portugal. I enjoy turning ideas into products and acting as the bridge between technical and business teams. I currently lead the software development team at Kito Health.
about.paragraph2: I graduated in IT from Unit (Universidade Tiradentes) in 2019. A lifelong gamer at heart, I bring that same curiosity and playfulness into crafting great software.

skills.lead: The stack I use to design, build and ship.

projects.title: Projects
projects.lead: Personal projects I've designed, built and shipped.

items.name: 7 Seconds
items.desc: Responsive multimedia website I built individually within a group project for the Multimedia Lab at the University of Porto.

items.name: Through My Eyes
items.desc: Another responsive multimedia site crafted solo within a group project for the Master in Multimedia at the University of Porto.

items.name: Evasion Predict
items.desc: Machine-learning application that predicts student dropout before it happens, targeting 80%+ accuracy. My final undergraduate thesis project.

items.name: Web Chat Bot
items.desc: Chatbot integration built on IBM Watson, connecting multiple student project groups through a shared conversational API.

work.title: Experience
work.lead: Where I've worked and what I built there.

items.desc: Own the full software life-cycle. I build reusable front-end and back-end components, turn designs and wireframes into working code, and optimize performance across mobile and web. Started as a Software Engineer in 2021 and stepped up to lead the team in 2022. (JavaScript, Node.js, React, Express, MongoDB, AWS)

Set the engineering standards for the startup (Git, AWS, Trello, Slack) and built its first responsive product in React. Later delivered a custom e-commerce platform, migrating from WooCommerce/PrestaShop to an in-house build with PHP, Laravel and MySQL.

Built features, fixed bugs and wrote unit tests for a major regional bank's software across front-end and back-end, working directly with the project's APIs in biweekly Scrum/Kanban sprints. (C#, ASP.NET, MVC)
Recorded and edited the studio's video content and researched new media tools to build the company's blog and website. (WordPress, Adobe Premiere)

cookies.title: Cookies & privacy
cookies.intro: This site keeps a couple of things in your browser to remember your preferences and, only if you allow it, calls third-party APIs to show your local weather. No ads, no tracking.

necessary.title: Essential
necessary.desc: Needed for the site to work and to remember your choices. Stored only in your browser and never sent anywhere.
necessary.item1: Language preference (portfolio-locale) — keeps the site in the language you picked.
necessary.item2: Cookie choice (portfolio-consent) — remembers this decision so you are not asked again.

weather.title: Weather & location
weather.desc: Powers the live weather scene in the home window. It sends your IP address to third-party services to estimate your approximate city and current weather. Decline and you simply get the default scene.
weather.item1: ipapi.co — turns your IP into an approximate location (city, coordinates).
weather.item2: open-meteo.com — returns the current weather for those coordinates.
weather.item3: wttr.in — backup weather + location if the first service is unavailable.
